text:
Proactive monitoring of the residual level to guide treatment optimization.
	
Anti-TNF and immunomodulator combination therapy
6	
Stopping the immunosuppressant should be discussed after 6 to 12 months of combination therapy if the residual infliximab level is within the target range and therapeutic targets are achieved. In anti-TNF–naïve patients receiving adalimumab, monotherapy is an alternative to combination therapy.
7	
In patients previously sensitized to IFX or in high-risk patients, combination therapy appears more prudent. In patients with active disease on anti-TNF, it is recommended to proactively monitor the residual level of IFX or ADA to guide therapeutic changes: treatment optimization or "switch".
8	
In patients on anti-TNF in whom remission is not achieved or maintained despite treatment optimization and association with an immunomodulator, treatment with ustekinumab or vedolizumab may be considered.
	
Adjunctive antibiotic therapy in perianal fistulizing Crohn’s disease
9	
Metronidazole 30 mg/kg/day, orally in 2 to 3 doses + ciprofloxacin
